prerequisites: An active 3PL Warehouse Manager (Extensiv, formerly 3PL Central) account with a warehouse that uses the platform.
REST API access requested for that warehouse. API keys are issued by the warehouse, who submits a request for REST API access to their Extensiv / 3PL Central representative.
The required connection values: client_id, client_secret, and the per-tenant API base host (url_base).
If filtering data, the optional customer_id and/or facility_id you intend to pull, plus a start_date for incremental backfill.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (Base64-encoded Client ID and Client Secret)
authMethods.summary: Authentication uses an OAuth 2.0 client-credentials flow. Provide client_id and client_secret (the only secret field). The connector joins them as 'client_id:client_secret', Base64-encodes the string, and sends it as an HTTP Basic Authorization header to the token endpoint to obtain a bearer access token. The access token is short-lived and is refreshed automatically. The optional user_login / user_login_id identifies which connection created a transaction for auditing; the optional tpl_key (TPL GUID) and customer_id / facility_id scope which warehouse data is returned. These keys are provided by your warehouse, not self-served.

setupSteps.title: Request REST API access from your warehouse
setupSteps.body: 3PL Warehouse Manager API credentials are not self-service. Ask the warehouse you work with to request REST API access from their Extensiv (3PL Central) representative. They will provide you a unique set of keys: client_id, client_secret, and the API base host. They may also provide a tpl_key (TPL GUID) and a user_login / user_login_id used to identify your connection.

setupSteps.title: Set the base host and credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter url_base as the API host issued for your tenant (for example the Extensiv API host). Enter client_id and the secret client_secret exactly as provided. The connector Base64-encodes 'client_id:client_secret' and exchanges it for an access token automatically; you do not generate the token yourself.

setupSteps.title: Provide connection and scoping identifiers
setupSteps.body: If your warehouse issued them, set user_login and user_login_id (used so transactions can be traced back to this connection) and tpl_key (your TPL GUID). Set customer_id and/or facility_id to scope extraction to a specific customer or facility when needed.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which records should be synced. The connector uses it as the lower bound for incremental backfill of time-based streams.

sources.title: Providing REST API Access - Extensiv Help
